T0048.title: Card Bust-Out
T0048.definition: A form of credit card fraud in which the cardholder deliberately maxes out the credit line through spending, cash advances, or installment plans and then intentionally defaults on all repayment obligations.
T0048.description: Operators exhaust the entire credit limit through large purchases, cash-out schemes, or installment-based consumption and then refuse to make any payments. Common methods include applying for cards with synthetic identities and draining them immediately, or using cyclic cash-out techniques to funnel the funds into illegal activities. This practice directly causes bad-debt losses for the issuing institution and is a classic modus operandi in credit card fraud.
T0048.usageExample: All cards obtained from that batch of identities are treated as empty-card runs—drain them and dump them. No one thinks about repayment.
